concerns.text: T015 ('Integrate novel object generation with evaluation loop') is listed as an implementation task within US1, but the actual generation of the 30 novel objects (FR-003) is not explicitly scheduled as a distinct, executable task prior to T013 (evaluate.py) or T014 (analysis.py). T007 only creates the *generator class*. Without a specific task to *execute* the generator and write the 30 geometry files to `data/generated/` before the evaluation phase, the consumer tasks (T013, T014) will fail due to missing input artifacts. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' dependency rule.

T012 (train.py) and T013 (evaluate.py) are marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T013 (evaluation) logically depends on the output of T012 (training) if the evaluation is meant to test the *trained* adaptive policy against a baseline. If T013 is intended to run a static baseline only, the dependency is unclear. If T013 requires the model weights produced by T012, they cannot run in parallel. The [P] tag is likely incorrect or the task descriptions lack the necessary dependency clarification.

T008 ('Configure random seed fixation') is marked [P]. While seed configuration is often a setup step, if this task involves writing to a shared state file or modifying global configuration that T004-T007 rely on, it creates a hidden ordering dependency. Ensure T008 is truly independent of the specific logic in T004-T007 to justify the [P] tag.
